description_long: This is a short, weathered wooden fence with a rustic and dilapidated appearance, likely used as a temporary or makeshift barrier in an urban or park setting. The fence consists of four vertical posts and three horizontal rails, with two of the rails broken and angled irregularly, suggesting damage or neglect. The wood is aged, showing signs of discoloration, moss, and surface roughness, with a grayish-brown hue. The top edge of the fence is flat, and the posts have no decorative caps or finials. There are no visible fasteners or hardware, and the construction appears simple and utilitarian. The gaps and broken rails make the fence easily traversable by people or small animals.
description: A short, weathered wooden fence with broken rails and a rustic, neglected look. The wood is grayish-brown, rough, and aged, with no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four posts, three horizontal rails, and two broken rails angled irregularly.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the protruding broken rails.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the broken rails and overall structure from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness and the broken rails extending outward.
